attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- less-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- claude-md-management:revise-claude-md: Update CLAUDE.md with learnings from this session
- commit-commands:commit-push-pr: Commit, push, and open a PR
- commit-commands:clean_gone: Cleans up all git branches marked as [gone] (branches that have been deleted on the remote but still exist locally), including removing associated worktrees.
- commit-commands:commit: Create a git commit
- hookify:help: Get help with the hookify plugin
- hookify:list: List all configured hookify rules
- hookify:configure: Enable or disable hookify rules interactively
- hookify:hookify: Create hooks to prevent unwanted behaviors from conversation analysis or explicit instructions
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- claude-md-management:claude-md-improver: Audit and improve CLAUDE.md files in repositories. Use when user asks to check, audit, update, improve, or fix CLAUDE.md files. Scans for all CLAUDE.md files, evaluates quality against templates, outputs quality report, then makes targeted updates. Also use when the user mentions "CLAUDE.md maintenance" or "project memory optimization".
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
- hookify:writing-rules: This skill should be used when the user asks to "create a hookify rule", "write a hook rule", "configure hookify", "add a hookify rule", or needs guidance on hookify rule syntax and patterns.
- chrome-devtools-mcp:debug-optimize-lcp: Guides debugging and optimizing Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) using Chrome DevTools MCP tools. Use this skill whenever the user asks about LCP performance, slow page loads, Core Web Vitals optimization, or wants to understand why their page's main content takes too long to appear. Also use when the user mentions "largest contentful paint", "page load speed", "CWV", or wants to improve how fast their hero image or main content renders.
- chrome-devtools-mcp:chrome-devtools: Uses Chrome DevTools via MCP for efficient debugging, troubleshooting and browser automation. Use when debugging web pages, automating browser interactions, analyzing performance, or inspecting network requests. This skill does not apply to `--slim` mode (MCP configuration).
- chrome-devtools-mcp:troubleshooting: Uses Chrome DevTools MCP and documentation to troubleshoot connection and target issues. Trigger this skill when list_pages, new_page, or navigate_page fail, or when the server initialization fails.
- chrome-devtools-mcp:a11y-debugging: Uses Chrome DevTools MCP for accessibility (a11y) debugging and auditing based on web.dev guidelines. Use when testing semantic HTML, ARIA labels, focus states, keyboard navigation, tap targets, and color contrast.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

" Auto-install vim-plug
let data_dir = has('nvim') ? stdpath('data') . '/site' : '~/.vim'
if empty(glob(data_dir . '/autoload/plug.vim'))
  silent execute '!curl -fLo '.data_dir.'/autoload/plug.vim --create-dirs  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junegunn/vim-plug/master/plug.vim'
  autocmd VimEnter * PlugInstall --sync | source $MYVIMRC
endif

" Leader
let mapleader = " "

set termguicolors
set number                " turn on line numbers
set nowrap                " don't wrap lines that go over a screens width
set cursorline            " highlight the line the cursor is on
set showmatch
set incsearch             " search as characters are entered
set hlsearch              " highlight matches when searching
set autoread              " Set to auto read when a file is changed from the outside
set lazyredraw            " Don't redraw while executing macros (good performance config)
set nrformats=            " treat all numbers as decimal not octal
set noswapfile            " don't create swap files
set smartcase             " case insensitive search
set mouse=a               " turn the mouse on
set spell spelllang=en_us " spell checking on
set complete+=kspell

:cab f FZF
:cab t Tags

" Fugitive short cuts
:cab gb Gblame

syntax on             " Enable syntax highlighting
filetype on           " Enable filetype detection
filetype indent on    " Enable filetype-specific indenting
filetype plugin on    " Enable filetype-specific plugins
runtime macros/matchit.vim

set expandtab
set tabstop=2 shiftwidth=2 softtabstop=2
set autoindent
set hidden

" Open new split panes to right and bottom, which feels more natural
set splitbelow
set splitright

" Quicker window movement
nnoremap <C-j> <C-w>j
nnoremap <C-k> <C-w>k
nnoremap <C-h> <C-w>h
nnoremap <C-l> <C-w>l

" Quicker window movement inside terminal session
tnoremap <C-j> <C-\><C-n><C-w>j
tnoremap <C-k> <C-\><C-n><C-w>k
tnoremap <C-h> <C-\><C-n><C-w>h
tnoremap <C-l> <C-\><C-n><C-w>l

" pbcopy clipboard sharing with MacOS
vnoremap \y y:call system("pbcopy", getreg("\""))<CR>
nnoremap \p :call setreg("\"", system("pbpaste"))<CR>p

noremap YY "+y<CR>
noremap P "+gP<CR>
noremap XX "+x<CR>

" Open NERDTree on CTRL + N
map <C-n> :NERDTreeToggle<CR>

" Display extra whitespace
set list listchars=tab:»·,trail:·,nbsp:·

" Use one space, not two, after punctuation.
set nojoinspaces

" Make it obvious where 100 characters is
set textwidth=100
set colorcolumn=+1

" Tab completion
" will insert tab at beginning of line,
" will use completion if not at beginning
set wildmode=list:longest,list:full
function! InsertTabWrapper()
    let col = col('.') - 1
    if !col || getline('.')[col - 1] !~ '\k'
        return "\<Tab>"
    else
        return "\<C-p>"
    endif
endfunction
inoremap <Tab> <C-r>=InsertTabWrapper()<CR>
inoremap <S-Tab> <C-n>

" Switch between the last two files
nnoremap <Leader><Leader> <C-^>

" Get off my lawn
nnoremap <Left> :echoe "Use h"<CR>
nnoremap <Right> :echoe "Use l"<CR>
nnoremap <Up> :echoe "Use k"<CR>

" Always use vertical diffs
set diffopt+=vertical

call plug#begin('~/.vim/plugged')

Plug 'morhetz/gruvbox', { 'as': 'gruvbox' }

Plug 'junegunn/fzf', { 'dir': '~/.fzf', 'do': './install --all' }
Plug 'junegunn/fzf.vim'

Plug 'jlanzarotta/bufexplorer'
Plug 'scrooloose/nerdtree'
Plug 'tpope/vim-commentary'
Plug 'djoshea/vim-autoread'
Plug 'https://github.com/tpope/vim-fugitive.git'
Plug 'prettier/vim-prettier'

call plug#end()

""""""""""""
"  Airline
""""""""""""
set laststatus=2
let g:airline#extensions#tabline#enabled = 1
let g:airline#extensions#tabline#fnamemod = ':t'
let g:airline_theme = 'gruvbox'

set background=dark
let g:gruvbox_contrast_dark = 'hard'

" Trim white space on save
let g:better_whitespace_enabled=1
let g:strip_whitespace_on_save=1


" NERDTress File highlighting
function! NERDTreeHighlightFile(extension, fg, bg, guifg, guibg)
 exec 'autocmd filetype nerdtree highlight ' . a:extension .' ctermbg='. a:bg .' ctermfg='. a:fg .' guibg='. a:guibg .' guifg='. a:guifg
 exec 'autocmd filetype nerdtree syn match ' . a:extension .' #^\s\+.*'. a:extension .'$#'
endfunction

let NERDTreeShowHidden=1

call NERDTreeHighlightFile('ini', 'yellow', 'none', 'yellow', '#151515')
call NERDTreeHighlightFile('yml', 'yellow', 'none', 'yellow', '#151515')
call NERDTreeHighlightFile('config', 'yellow', 'none', 'yellow', '#151515')
call NERDTreeHighlightFile('conf', 'yellow', 'none', 'yellow', '#151515')
call NERDTreeHighlightFile('json', 'yellow', 'none', 'yellow', '#151515')
call NERDTreeHighlightFile('html', 'yellow', 'none', 'yellow', '#151515')
call NERDTreeHighlightFile('css', 'cyan', 'none', 'cyan', '#151515')
call NERDTreeHighlightFile('js', 'Red', 'none', '#ffa500', '#151515')
call NERDTreeHighlightFile('rb', 'Magenta', 'none', '#ff00ff', '#151515')
call NERDTreeHighlightFile('ex', 'Magenta', 'none', '#ff00ff', '#151515')

" Colorscheme
colorscheme gruvbox

lastPrompt: look through my init.vim file, and see what can be improved/modernized about it

content.text:
Here's a review of `init.vim` — grouped by impact.

## Dead / broken config
- **Lines 126–129 (airline)** and **135–136 (better_whitespace)**: config is set but the plugins aren't in your `plug#begin` block. Either add the plugins or delete the config.
- **Line 31 (`:cab gb Gblame`)**: `Gblame` was renamed to `Git blame` in fugitive years ago. Update to `:cab gb Git blame`.

## Redundant in modern Neovim
- **Lines 33–37**: `syntax on`, `filetype on/indent/plugin on`, and `runtime macros/matchit.vim` are all on by default in Neovim. Delete.
- **Line 19 (`set lazyredraw`)**: no longer recommended in nvim — can cause rendering glitches with async plugins.
- **Lines 61–66 (pbcopy/pbpaste)**: replace with `set clipboard=unnamedplus` — nvim handles macOS clipboard natively.
- **`djoshea/vim-autoread` (line 117)**: drop it; `set autoread` + a tiny `FocusGained,BufEnter * checktime` autocmd covers it.
- **`tpope/vim-commentary` (line 116)**: nvim 0.10+ ships built-in `gc`/`gcc` commentary.

## Big modernization opportunities
- **Migrate to `init.lua`**. Lua config is faster, better typed, and the whole plugin ecosystem assumes it.
- **Plugin manager**: swap vim-plug → **lazy.nvim** (lazy loading, lockfile, UI).
- **Plugins path** `~/.vim/plugged` (line 107) should be under `stdpath('data')` on nvim.
- **NERDTree → `nvim-tree.lua`, `neo-tree.nvim`, or `oil.nvim`** (oil is great if you like editing directories as buffers).
- **FZF → `telescope.nvim`** (or `fzf-lua` if you want to keep fzf). Much better previews and pickers.
- **Add `nvim-treesitter`** for real syntax highlighting/indent.
- **Add LSP** via `nvim-lspconfig` + `mason.nvim`, with **`blink.cmp`** (or `nvim-cmp`) for completion — which lets you delete the `InsertTabWrapper` function (lines 85–94).
- **`prettier/vim-prettier` → `conform.nvim`** for unified formatting across languages.

## Smaller cleanups
- **Line 24 (`set spell` globally)**: consider scoping to filetypes (markdown, gitcommit) via autocmd — global spellcheck in code gets noisy.
- **Line 78 (`textwidth=100`)**: auto-wraps text on insert; if you only wanted the visual ruler, use `set colorcolumn=100` and drop `textwidth`.
- **Lines 100–102 ("Get off my lawn")**: missing `<Down>`. Either add it or drop the block.

Want me to draft a migrated `init.lua` with lazy.nvim + treesitter + LSP + telescope as a starting point?

message.content: do all those changes, but keep fzf for now, i'll want to try that out separately

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

brew update
brew upgrade

brew install git
brew install rg
brew install fd # we'll use FD for FZF to support .gitignore easily

# Install and setup environment for Python development
## Install PIPX and Poetry https://github.com/pypa/pipx
brew install pipx
pipx ensurepath
sudo pipx ensurepath --global # optional to allow pipx actions with --global argument
# https://python-poetry.org/docs/#installing-with-pipx
pipx install poetry
poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish

# Install node and setup environment for JS development
brew install node
brew install nvm
## Install neovim, vim plug and deoplete dependencies
brew install nvim
curl -fLo ~/.vim/autoload/plug.vim --create-dirs \
    https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junegunn/vim-plug/master/plug.vim
pip3 install --user pynvim

# Install/setup for ruby/rails development
curl https://mise.run | sh
brew install libyaml # https://stackoverflow.com/questions/78817340/mise-cannot-install-ruby3-2-1-on-mac-m3
mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby
eval "$(mise activate bash)"
eval "$(mise activate zsh)"
mise activate fish | source

# Install fish and fisher
brew install fish
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source && fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher

# Fish plugins
fisher install jorgebucaran/nvm.fish
fisher install jethrokuan/z

# Install Oh-My-Fish (omf)
curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oh-my-fish/oh-my-fish/master/bin/install | fish

# Install fisher theme
omf install clearance
omf reload
omf theme clearance

# Install and configure fzf
brew install fzf
$(brew --prefix)/opt/fzf/install

# Install tmux + oh-my-tmux
brew install tmux
git clone https://github.com/gpakosz/.tmux.git ~/oh-my-tmux
ln -s -f ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Install alacritty
brew install --cask alacritty
mkdir -p ~/.config/alacritty
cp alacritty.toml ~/.config/alacritty/

# Fonts
brew install --cask font-fira-code

# Java
brew install openjdk

# Docker
brew install --cask docker

# 1password
brew install --cask 1password

# VLC
brew install --cask vlc

# Install Claude Code tmux notification scripts
mkdir -p ~/.claude/scripts
cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh ~/.claude/scripts/
cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh ~/.claude/scripts/
chmod +x ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh

# Add Claude Code Stop hook to ~/.claude/settings.json (creates file if missing)
python3 - <<'PYEOF'
import json, os

path = os.path.expanduser("~/.claude/settings.json")
settings = {}
if os.path.exists(path):
    with open(path) as f:
        settings = json.load(f)

hook = {"type": "command", "command": "bash ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh"}
stop_hooks = settings.setdefault("hooks", {}).setdefault("Stop", [])

# Only add if not already present
if not any(h.get("hooks", []) == [hook] for h in stop_hooks):
    stop_hooks.append({"hooks": [hook]})

with open(path, "w") as f:
    json.dump(settings, f, indent=2)
    f.write("\n")

print("Claude settings.json updated with tmux Stop hook")
PYEOF

-- Leader must be set before lazy.nvim loads so plugin mappings pick it up
vim.g.mapleader = " "
vim.g.maplocalleader = " "

-- Bootstrap lazy.nvim
local lazypath = vim.fn.stdpath("data") .. "/lazy/lazy.nvim"
if not (vim.uv or vim.loop).fs_stat(lazypath) then
  local out = vim.fn.system({
    "git",
    "clone",
    "--filter=blob:none",
    "--branch=stable",
    "https://github.com/folke/lazy.nvim.git",
    lazypath,
  })
  if vim.v.shell_error ~= 0 then
    vim.api.nvim_echo({
      { "Failed to clone lazy.nvim:\n", "ErrorMsg" },
      { out, "WarningMsg" },
    }, true, {})
    vim.fn.getchar()
    os.exit(1)
  end
end
vim.opt.rtp:prepend(lazypath)

require("config.options")
require("config.keymaps")
require("config.autocmds")

require("lazy").setup({
  spec = { { import = "plugins" } },
  change_detection = { notify = false },
})

local map = vim.keymap.set

-- Window navigation
map("n", "<C-h>", "<C-w>h")
map("n", "<C-j>", "<C-w>j")
map("n", "<C-k>", "<C-w>k")
map("n", "<C-l>", "<C-w>l")

-- Terminal-mode window navigation
map("t", "<C-h>", [[<C-\><C-n><C-w>h]])
map("t", "<C-j>", [[<C-\><C-n><C-w>j]])
map("t", "<C-k>", [[<C-\><C-n><C-w>k]])
map("t", "<C-l>", [[<C-\><C-n><C-w>l]])

-- Switch to alternate buffer
map("n", "<Leader><Leader>", "<C-^>")

-- Get off my lawn
for key, hint in pairs({ Left = "h", Down = "j", Up = "k", Right = "l" }) do
  map("n", "<" .. key .. ">", function()
    vim.notify("Use " .. hint, vim.log.levels.WARN)
  end)
end

#!/bin/bash

brew update
brew upgrade

brew install git
brew install rg
brew install fd # we'll use FD for FZF to support .gitignore easily

# Install and setup environment for Python development
## Install PIPX and Poetry https://github.com/pypa/pipx
brew install pipx
pipx ensurepath
sudo pipx ensurepath --global # optional to allow pipx actions with --global argument
# https://python-poetry.org/docs/#installing-with-pipx
pipx install poetry
poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish

# Install node and setup environment for JS development
brew install node
brew install nvm
## Install neovim, vim plug and deoplete dependencies
brew install nvim
curl -fLo ~/.vim/autoload/plug.vim --create-dirs \
    https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junegunn/vim-plug/master/plug.vim
pip3 install --user pynvim

# Install/setup for ruby/rails development
curl https://mise.run | sh
brew install libyaml # https://stackoverflow.com/questions/78817340/mise-cannot-install-ruby3-2-1-on-mac-m3
mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby
eval "$(mise activate bash)"
eval "$(mise activate zsh)"
mise activate fish | source

# Install fish and fisher
brew install fish
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source && fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher

# Fish plugins
fisher install jorgebucaran/nvm.fish
fisher install jethrokuan/z

# Install Oh-My-Fish (omf)
curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oh-my-fish/oh-my-fish/master/bin/install | fish

# Install fisher theme
omf install clearance
omf reload
omf theme clearance

# Install and configure fzf
brew install fzf
$(brew --prefix)/opt/fzf/install

# Install tmux + oh-my-tmux
brew install tmux
git clone https://github.com/gpakosz/.tmux.git ~/oh-my-tmux
ln -s -f ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Fonts
brew install --cask font-fira-code

# Java
brew install openjdk

# Docker
brew install --cask docker

# 1password
brew install --cask 1password

# VLC
brew install --cask vlc

# Claude Code quality of life
brew install rtk
claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman
claude plugin install caveman@caveman

# Install Claude Code tmux notification scripts
mkdir -p ~/.claude/scripts
cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh ~/.claude/scripts/
cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh ~/.claude/scripts/
chmod +x ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh

import json, os

path = os.path.expanduser("~/.claude/settings.json")
settings = {}
if os.path.exists(path):
    with open(path) as f:
        settings = json.load(f)

hook = {"type": "command", "command": "bash ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh"}
stop_hooks = settings.setdefault("hooks", {}).setdefault("Stop", [])

# Only add if not already present
if not any(h.get("hooks", []) == [hook] for h in stop_hooks):
    stop_hooks.append({"hooks": [hook]})

with open(path, "w") as f:
    json.dump(settings, f, indent=2)
    f.write("\n")

print("Claude settings.json updated with tmux Stop hook")
PYEOF

content.text:
All three config modules loaded cleanly in a headless nvim. Final layout:

```
init.lua
lua/
  config/{options,keymaps,autocmds}.lua
  plugins/{colorscheme,fzf,bufexplorer,fugitive,filetree,
           lualine,treesitter,lsp,completion,conform}.lua
```

**What changed vs. the old init.vim:**

| Area | Old | New |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Config lang | vimscript | Lua |
| Plugin mgr | vim-plug | **lazy.nvim** (auto-bootstraps) |
| Colors | morhetz/gruvbox | ellisonleao/gruvbox.nvim |
| Tree | NERDTree + highlight hacks | **nvim-tree** |
| Statusline | airline config (plugin wasn't even installed) | **lualine** |
| Syntax | `syntax on` only | **nvim-treesitter** |
| LSP | none | **nvim-lspconfig + mason** |
| Completion | `InsertTabWrapper` fn | **blink.cmp** (super-tab preset) |
| Formatter | vim-prettier | **conform.nvim** (prettier/stylua/rubocop/ruff) |
| Clipboard | pbcopy/pbpaste mappings | `clipboard=unnamedplus` |
| Autoread | djoshea/vim-autoread | `checktime` autocmd |
| Commentary | tpope/vim-commentary | built-in `gc`/`gcc` |
| Trim WS | better_whitespace config (plugin missing) | `BufWritePre` autocmd |
| `:Gblame` cabbrev | broken | `:Git blame` |
| `set spell` | global (noisy in code) | scoped to markdown/gitcommit/text |
| `set lazyredraw` | set | removed |
| `syntax on`/`filetype *`/`matchit` | set | removed (nvim defaults) |
| `set incsearch/hlsearch/autoread/hidden/autoindent` | set | removed (nvim defaults) |
| `textwidth=100` (auto-wraps) | set | dropped; kept `colorcolumn=101` |
| `smartcase` without `ignorecase` | no-op | fixed by enabling both |
| Arrow keys | missing `<Down>` | added |
| install-core.sh | downloaded vim-plug | copies `init.lua`+`lua/` to `~/.config/nvim`, removes stale `init.vim` |

**To try it now without rerunning the installer:**

```
rm ~/.config/nvim/init.vim
cp -R ~/src/dotfiles/init.lua ~/src/dotfiles/lua ~/.config/nvim/
nvim   # lazy.nvim will bootstrap itself and install everything
```

On first launch, run `:Mason` to confirm language servers are installing and `:checkhealth` to verify the stack.

content.text:
Fixed. Layout is now `~/.config/nvim/{init.lua, lua/config/*, lua/plugins/*}`. Try `nvim` again — lazy.nvim will bootstrap on first launch.

(The cp source probably got a trailing slash, which copies dir *contents* instead of the dir itself. The `install-core.sh` uses `cp -R lua ~/.config/nvim/` without the trailing slash so a fresh install wouldn't hit this.)

If you want to silence the warning, add `rocks = { enabled = false }` to the lazy setup:

```
brew install --cask font-fira-code-nerd-font
```

**2. Point Alacritty at it** — update `alacritty.toml`:

content.text:
Yes — the `?` glyphs are missing Nerd Font icons used by `nvim-web-devicons` (pulled in by lualine and nvim-tree). You have plain Fira Code; you need the Nerd Font variant.

Options:

1. **Install a Nerd Font** and point your terminal at it:
   ```
   brew install --cask font-fira-code-nerd-font
   ```
   Then change `family = "Fira Code"` → `family = "FiraCode Nerd Font"` in `alacritty.toml`.

2. **Disable icons** in nvim (no font change needed) — set `icons_enabled = false` in `lua/plugins/lualine.lua` `options`, and remove `nvim-web-devicons` from filetree/lualine deps.

I'd go with option 1 — Nerd Fonts are useful beyond nvim (tmux statuslines, `eza`, `starship`, etc.) and most modern nvim plugins assume them.

Should I also add the nerd-font install to `install-core.sh` (replacing the plain Fira Code cask)?

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

brew update
brew upgrade

brew install git
brew install rg
brew install fd # we'll use FD for FZF to support .gitignore easily

# Install and setup environment for Python development
## Install PIPX and Poetry https://github.com/pypa/pipx
brew install pipx
pipx ensurepath
sudo pipx ensurepath --global # optional to allow pipx actions with --global argument
# https://python-poetry.org/docs/#installing-with-pipx
pipx install poetry
poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish

# Install node and setup environment for JS development
brew install node
brew install nvm
## Install neovim
brew install nvim
pip3 install --user pynvim

# Install nvim config (lazy.nvim auto-installs on first launch)
mkdir -p ~/.config/nvim
cp init.lua ~/.config/nvim/
cp -R lua ~/.config/nvim/
# Remove any leftover init.vim so nvim loads init.lua
rm -f ~/.config/nvim/init.vim

# Install/setup for ruby/rails development
curl https://mise.run | sh
brew install libyaml # https://stackoverflow.com/questions/78817340/mise-cannot-install-ruby3-2-1-on-mac-m3
mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby
eval "$(mise activate bash)"
eval "$(mise activate zsh)"
mise activate fish | source

# Install fish and fisher
brew install fish
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source && fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher

# Fish plugins
fisher install jorgebucaran/nvm.fish
fisher install jethrokuan/z

# Install Oh-My-Fish (omf)
curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oh-my-fish/oh-my-fish/master/bin/install | fish

# Install fisher theme
omf install clearance
omf reload
omf theme clearance

# Install and configure fzf
brew install fzf
$(brew --prefix)/opt/fzf/install

# Install tmux + oh-my-tmux
brew install tmux
git clone https://github.com/gpakosz/.tmux.git ~/oh-my-tmux
ln -s -f ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Fonts
brew install --cask font-fira-code

# Java
brew install openjdk

# Docker
brew install --cask docker

# 1password
brew install --cask 1password

# VLC
brew install --cask vlc

# Claude Code quality of life
brew install rtk
claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman
claude plugin install caveman@caveman

# Install Claude Code tmux notification scripts
mkdir -p ~/.claude/scripts
cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh ~/.claude/scripts/
cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh ~/.claude/scripts/
chmod +x ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh

# Add Claude Code Stop hook to ~/.claude/settings.json (creates file if missing)
python3 - <<'PYEOF'
import json, os

path = os.path.expanduser("~/.claude/settings.json")
settings = {}
if os.path.exists(path):
    with open(path) as f:
        settings = json.load(f)

hook = {"type": "command", "command": "bash ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh"}
stop_hooks = settings.setdefault("hooks", {}).setdefault("Stop", [])

# Only add if not already present
if not any(h.get("hooks", []) == [hook] for h in stop_hooks):
    stop_hooks.append({"hooks": [hook]})

with open(path, "w") as f:
    json.dump(settings, f, indent=2)
    f.write("\n")

print("Claude settings.json updated with tmux Stop hook")
PYEOF

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Migrate nvim config from init.vim to init.lua with lazy.nvim

Replace vim-plug/init.vim with a modular Lua config managed by
lazy.nvim. Adds treesitter, nvim-lspconfig + mason, blink.cmp,
conform.nvim, nvim-tree, and lualine; drops vim-commentary,
vim-autoread, vim-prettier, and NERDTree (and their dead airline /
better_whitespace config). Fixes the stale :Gblame cabbrev, scopes
spell checking to prose filetypes, and uses clipboard=unnamedplus
instead of manual pbcopy/pbpaste mappings.

Also: switch to font-fira-code-nerd-font for devicon glyphs and
remove the alacritty.toml config now that alacritty is no longer
used.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git status

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user asked for a review of their `init.vim` to identify what could be improved/modernized. After receiving the review, they requested all recommended changes be implemented while keeping FZF (they want to evaluate telescope separately). Subsequent requests included: diagnosing a lua module loading error, asking about a luarocks warning, addressing missing font glyphs (`?` characters), updating `install-core.sh` with a Nerd Font and removing alacritty references (user had removed alacritty), and finally committing all changes.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Neovim Lua configuration (init.lua) replacing vimscript (init.vim)
   - lazy.nvim plugin manager (replaces vim-plug)
   - Modular config structure: `lua/config/` + `lua/plugins/`
   - nvim-treesitter for syntax highlighting
   - LSP via nvim-lspconfig + mason.nvim + mason-lspconfig.nvim
   - blink.cmp for completion (super-tab preset replacing InsertTabWrapper)
   - conform.nvim for formatting (replaces vim-prettier)
   - nvim-tree.lua (replaces NERDTree)
   - lualine.nvim (replaces dead airline config)
   - gruvbox.nvim (Lua port, replaces morhetz/gruvbox)
   - Autocmds for auto-reload, scoped spell check, trim trailing whitespace, highlight-on-yank
   - `clipboard=unnamedplus` replacing pbcopy/pbpaste mappings
   - Nerd Fonts required for nvim-web-devicons glyphs

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `/Users/anton/src/dotfiles/init.vim` (read, then DELETED)
     - Original 160-line vim config used as basis for migration
   - `/Users/anton/src/dotfiles/init.lua` (CREATED)
     - Bootstraps lazy.nvim, sets leader to space BEFORE lazy loads, requires config modules
     - Final version includes `rocks = { enabled = false }` to silence luarocks warning
   - `/Users/anton/src/dotfiles/lua/config/options.lua` (CREATED)
     - Removed nvim defaults (incsearch/hlsearch/autoread/hidden/autoindent/joinspaces)
     - Added `ignorecase=true` + `smartcase=true` (fix for smartcase being no-op without ignorecase)
     - `clipboard="unnamedplus"`, `laststatus=3`, `colorcolumn="101"`

#!/bin/bash

brew update
brew upgrade

brew install git
brew install rg
brew install fd # we'll use FD for FZF to support .gitignore easily

# Install and setup environment for Python development
## Install PIPX and Poetry https://github.com/pypa/pipx
brew install pipx
pipx ensurepath
sudo pipx ensurepath --global # optional to allow pipx actions with --global argument
# https://python-poetry.org/docs/#installing-with-pipx
pipx install poetry
poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish

# Install node and setup environment for JS development
brew install node
brew install nvm
## Install neovim
brew install nvim
pip3 install --user pynvim

# Install nvim config (lazy.nvim auto-installs on first launch)
mkdir -p ~/.config/nvim
cp init.lua ~/.config/nvim/
cp -R lua ~/.config/nvim/
# Remove any leftover init.vim so nvim loads init.lua
rm -f ~/.config/nvim/init.vim

# Install/setup for ruby/rails development
curl https://mise.run | sh
brew install libyaml # https://stackoverflow.com/questions/78817340/mise-cannot-install-ruby3-2-1-on-mac-m3
mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby
eval "$(mise activate bash)"
eval "$(mise activate zsh)"
mise activate fish | source

# Install fish and fisher
brew install fish
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source && fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher

# Fish plugins
fisher install jorgebucaran/nvm.fish
fisher install jethrokuan/z

# Install Oh-My-Fish (omf)
curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oh-my-fish/oh-my-fish/master/bin/install | fish

# Install fisher theme
omf install clearance
omf reload
omf theme clearance

# Install and configure fzf
brew install fzf
$(brew --prefix)/opt/fzf/install

# Install tmux + oh-my-tmux
brew install tmux
git clone https://github.com/gpakosz/.tmux.git ~/oh-my-tmux
ln -s -f ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Fonts (Nerd Font variant for nvim-web-devicons / lualine glyphs)
brew install --cask font-fira-code-nerd-font

# Java
brew install openjdk

# Docker
brew install --cask docker

# 1password
brew install --cask 1password

# VLC
brew install --cask vlc

# Claude Code
curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# Claude Code quality of life
brew install rtk
claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman
claude plugin install caveman@caveman

# Install Claude Code tmux notification scripts
mkdir -p ~/.claude/scripts
cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh ~/.claude/scripts/
cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh ~/.claude/scripts/
chmod +x ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh

# Add Claude Code Stop hook to ~/.claude/settings.json (creates file if missing)
python3 - <<'PYEOF'
import json, os

path = os.path.expanduser("~/.claude/settings.json")
settings = {}
if os.path.exists(path):
    with open(path) as f:
        settings = json.load(f)

hook = {"type": "command", "command": "bash ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh"}
stop_hooks = settings.setdefault("hooks", {}).setdefault("Stop", [])

# Only add if not already present
if not any(h.get("hooks", []) == [hook] for h in stop_hooks):
    stop_hooks.append({"hooks": [hook]})

with open(path, "w") as f:
    json.dump(settings, f, indent=2)
    f.write("\n")

print("Claude settings.json updated with tmux Stop hook")
PYEOF

-- Leader must be set before lazy.nvim loads so plugin mappings pick it up
vim.g.mapleader = " "
vim.g.maplocalleader = " "

-- Bootstrap lazy.nvim
local lazypath = vim.fn.stdpath("data") .. "/lazy/lazy.nvim"
if not (vim.uv or vim.loop).fs_stat(lazypath) then
  local out = vim.fn.system({
    "git",
    "clone",
    "--filter=blob:none",
    "--branch=stable",
    "https://github.com/folke/lazy.nvim.git",
    lazypath,
  })
  if vim.v.shell_error ~= 0 then
    vim.api.nvim_echo({
      { "Failed to clone lazy.nvim:\n", "ErrorMsg" },
      { out, "WarningMsg" },
    }, true, {})
    vim.fn.getchar()
    os.exit(1)
  end
end
vim.opt.rtp:prepend(lazypath)

require("config.options")
require("config.keymaps")
require("config.autocmds")

require("lazy").setup({
  spec = { { import = "plugins" } },
  change_detection = { notify = false },
  rocks = { enabled = false },
})

content.text:
That's a treesitter parser/nvim ABI mismatch — the error is in nvim's built-in runtime treesitter code (`vim/treesitter/languagetree.lua:215`) calling `range` on a nil tree, which happens when an installed parser was compiled against an older nvim and returns a malformed tree on 0.12.1.

Fix: run `:TSUpdate` in nvim to rebuild all parsers against the current version, then restart. If one specific parser is broken, `:checkhealth nvim-treesitter` will point at it and you can `:TSUninstall <lang>` + `:TSInstall <lang>` that one.

content.text:
Try `:checkhealth` with no arg (lists everything) or `:checkhealth vim.treesitter`. But simplest path: just run `:TSUpdate` and restart nvim — that rebuilds all parsers and usually clears the error.

If it still happens, open a file where it reproduces and run `:=vim.treesitter.language.get_lang(vim.bo.filetype)` to see which parser is active — we can target that one specifically.

## Install neovim

# Install nvim config (lazy.nvim auto-installs on first launch)

cp init.lua ~/.config/nvim/

# Fish plugins

omf theme clearance

git clone https://github.com/gpakosz/.tmux.git ~/oh-my-tmux
ln -s -f ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Fonts (Nerd Font variant for nvim-web-devicons / lualine glyphs)
brew install --cask font-fira-code-nerd-font

# Java
brew install openjdk

# Docker
brew install --cask docker

# 1password

# VLC

require("config.options")

lastPrompt: review my install-core.sh script to see what can be improved/modernized

content.text:
I already have the file from the prior read. Here's a review grouped by priority.

## Bugs / breakage risk

- **`pip3 install --user pynvim` (line 24)** — on modern brew Python this hits PEP 668 "externally-managed environment" and fails. Use `pipx install pynvim` (you already have pipx). Or drop it entirely — most plugins don't need the python provider anymore.
- **`mise activate` eval lines (36-39)** — these only affect the running script's shell; they don't persist. Either remove them, or instead append the activation to `~/.config/fish/config.fish`, `~/.zshrc`, `~/.bashrc`. As written they're dead weight.
- **`poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish`** — will fail if the directory doesn't exist. Prepend `mkdir -p ~/.config/fish/completions`.
- **`git clone ~/oh-my-tmux`** — fails on re-run. Guard with `[ -d ~/oh-my-tmux ] || git clone …`.
- **No `set -euo pipefail`** — silent partial failure. A pipx install could fail, script keeps chugging, you think it succeeded.
- **`sudo pipx ensurepath --global`** — mixing sudo pipx with user pipx is a footgun and rarely needed on a single-user dev machine. Drop unless you actually need `--global` behavior.

## Redundancy / conceptual overlap

- **Three node managers**: `brew install node` + `brew install nvm` + `fisher install jorgebucaran/nvm.fish`. Pick one. If you use mise (which you do), delete all three and `mise use -g node@lts`.
- **`oh-my-fish` (line 50)** is largely unmaintained. The `clearance` theme is the only reason it's here. Worth replacing with a maintained fish prompt (tide, starship) if you want to modernize — otherwise leave it.
- **`rg` should be `ripgrep`** (line 7) — `rg` is an alias that works today but `ripgrep` is the canonical formula name; aliases occasionally get removed.

## Idempotency

The whole script assumes a fresh machine. Re-running breaks on the `git clone`, the `cp` overwrites the tmux local config silently, and the fisher/omf installers may prompt or error. Two cleaner paths:

1. **Brewfile**: move every `brew install …` / `brew install --cask …` into a `Brewfile` and call `brew bundle --file=Brewfile`. Declarative, diffable, idempotent.
2. **Guards**: wrap each non-brew step in `command -v X >/dev/null || …` or `[ -e path ] || …`.

## Other

- **`brew update && brew upgrade`** at the top upgrades the user's entire brew tree every time this script runs. Fine on a fresh machine, surprising on re-run. Consider dropping `upgrade`.
- **Java (line 71)** — `brew install openjdk` doesn't symlink into the system Java wrappers. If you actually use it, you need `sudo ln -sfn $(brew --prefix)/opt/openjdk/libexec/openjdk.jdk /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk` and/or `JAVA_HOME` set in your shell config.
- **`fd` comment** — FZF honoring `.gitignore` requires `FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND='fd --type f --hidden --follow --exclude .git'` in your shell config. The comment implies install is enough; it's not.

Want me to apply any of these? I'd start with: `set -euo pipefail`, the `pip3 → pipx` fix, the `mkdir -p` before poetry completion, the `oh-my-tmux` idempotency guard, and converting the brew installs to a Brewfile.

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")"

# Homebrew packages (see Brewfile)
brew update
brew bundle --file=Brewfile

# Python tooling
pipx ensurepath
pipx install poetry
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish/completions
poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish

# Neovim config (lazy.nvim auto-installs on first launch)
mkdir -p ~/.config/nvim
cp init.lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -rf ~/.config/nvim/lua
cp -R lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -f ~/.config/nvim/init.vim

# mise: Ruby/Node/Python runtime manager
if ! command -v mise >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl https://mise.run | sh
fi
mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby || true
# NOTE: activate mise by adding to your shell configs:
#   bash:  eval "$(mise activate bash)"
#   zsh:   eval "$(mise activate zsh)"
#   fish:  mise activate fish | source
# Install toolchains as needed, e.g.: mise use -g node@lts ruby@3.3

# Fisher + fish plugins
if ! fish -c 'functions -q fisher' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  fish -c 'curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source; and fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher'
fi
fish -c 'fisher install jethrokuan/z'

# Oh-My-Fish (for the `clearance` theme)
if [ ! -d ~/.local/share/omf ]; then
  curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oh-my-fish/oh-my-fish/master/bin/install | fish
fi
fish -c 'omf install clearance; and omf theme clearance'

# fzf keybindings & shell integration
"$(brew --prefix)"/opt/fzf/install --all
# NOTE: add to your shell config so fzf respects .gitignore via fd:
#   export FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND='fd --type f --hidden --follow --exclude .git'

# tmux + oh-my-tmux
[ -d ~/oh-my-tmux ] || git clone https://github.com/gpakosz/.tmux.git ~/oh-my-tmux
ln -sf ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
[ -f ~/.tmux.conf.local ] || cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Java: link openjdk into the system JVM path
if [ ! -e /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk ]; then
  sudo ln -sfn "$(brew --prefix)/opt/openjdk/libexec/openjdk.jdk" \
    /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk
fi

# Claude Code
if ! command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
fi
claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman || true
claude plugin install caveman@caveman || true

#!/bin/bash

brew update
brew upgrade

brew install git
brew install rg
brew install fd # we'll use FD for FZF to support .gitignore easily

# Install and setup environment for Python development
## Install PIPX and Poetry https://github.com/pypa/pipx
brew install pipx
pipx ensurepath
sudo pipx ensurepath --global # optional to allow pipx actions with --global argument
# https://python-poetry.org/docs/#installing-with-pipx
pipx install poetry
poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish

# Install node and setup environment for JS development
brew install node
brew install nvm
## Install neovim
brew install nvim
pip3 install --user pynvim

# Install nvim config (lazy.nvim auto-installs on first launch)
mkdir -p ~/.config/nvim
cp init.lua ~/.config/nvim/
cp -R lua ~/.config/nvim/
# Remove any leftover init.vim so nvim loads init.lua
rm -f ~/.config/nvim/init.vim

# Install/setup for ruby/rails development
curl https://mise.run | sh
brew install libyaml # https://stackoverflow.com/questions/78817340/mise-cannot-install-ruby3-2-1-on-mac-m3
mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby
eval "$(mise activate bash)"
eval "$(mise activate zsh)"
mise activate fish | source

# Install fish and fisher
brew install fish
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source && fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher

# Fish plugins
fisher install jorgebucaran/nvm.fish
fisher install jethrokuan/z

# Install Oh-My-Fish (omf)
curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oh-my-fish/oh-my-fish/master/bin/install | fish

# Install fisher theme
omf install clearance
omf reload
omf theme clearance

# Install and configure fzf
brew install fzf
$(brew --prefix)/opt/fzf/install

# Install tmux + oh-my-tmux
brew install tmux
git clone https://github.com/gpakosz/.tmux.git ~/oh-my-tmux
ln -s -f ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Fonts (Nerd Font variant for nvim-web-devicons / lualine glyphs)
brew install --cask font-fira-code-nerd-font

# Java
brew install openjdk

# Docker
brew install --cask docker

# 1password
brew install --cask 1password

# VLC
brew install --cask vlc

# Claude Code
curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# Claude Code quality of life
brew install rtk
claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman
claude plugin install caveman@caveman

content.text:
Done. Two files changed:

**`Brewfile`** (new) — declarative package list. `ripgrep`/`neovim` use canonical names; `node`/`nvm` dropped (mise handles node).

**`install-core.sh`** — rewrite with:
- `set -euo pipefail` + `cd "$(dirname "$0")"` so it fails loudly and runs from anywhere
- `brew bundle --file=Brewfile` replaces all the individual `brew install` lines
- Dropped `pip3 install --user pynvim` (PEP 668 hazard; add back if a plugin actually needs it)
- Dropped dead `mise activate` eval lines; left a comment showing what to add to your shell configs instead
- Dropped `sudo pipx ensurepath --global`
- `mkdir -p ~/.config/fish/completions` before poetry completion dump
- `rm -rf ~/.config/nvim/lua` before `cp -R lua` (avoids nested `lua/lua` on re-run)
- `[ -d ~/oh-my-tmux ] || git clone …` idempotency guard
- `[ -f ~/.tmux.conf.local ] || cp …` so re-runs don't clobber local tmux edits
- Fisher bootstrap wrapped in `fish -c '…'` (original line was broken under `#!/bin/bash`)
- Java openjdk symlinked into `/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines` (idempotent)
- Claude Code install guarded by `command -v claude`; plugin commands `|| true`
- Inline comment reminding you to set `FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND` to use fd

Two things still live outside this script that you'll want in your shell configs: mise activation and `FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND`. Want me to add those to your fish/zsh configs too?

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")"

# Homebrew packages (see Brewfile)
brew update
brew bundle --file=Brewfile

# Python tooling
pipx ensurepath
pipx install poetry
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish/completions
poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish

# Neovim config (lazy.nvim auto-installs on first launch)
mkdir -p ~/.config/nvim
cp init.lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -rf ~/.config/nvim/lua
cp -R lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -f ~/.config/nvim/init.vim

# mise: Ruby/Node/Python runtime manager
if ! command -v mise >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl https://mise.run | sh
fi
mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby || true
# NOTE: activate mise by adding to your shell configs:
#   bash:  eval "$(mise activate bash)"
#   zsh:   eval "$(mise activate zsh)"
#   fish:  mise activate fish | source
# Install toolchains as needed, e.g.: mise use -g node@lts ruby@3.3

# Fisher + fish plugins
if ! fish -c 'functions -q fisher' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  fish -c 'curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source; and fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher'
fi
fish -c 'fisher install jethrokuan/z'

# Oh-My-Fish (for the `clearance` theme)
if [ ! -d ~/.local/share/omf ]; then
  curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oh-my-fish/oh-my-fish/master/bin/install | fish
fi
fish -c 'omf install clearance; and omf theme clearance'

# fzf keybindings & shell integration
"$(brew --prefix)"/opt/fzf/install --all
# NOTE: add to your shell config so fzf respects .gitignore via fd:
#   export FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND='fd --type f --hidden --follow --exclude .git'

# tmux + oh-my-tmux
[ -d ~/oh-my-tmux ] || git clone https://github.com/gpakosz/.tmux.git ~/oh-my-tmux
ln -sf ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
[ -f ~/.tmux.conf.local ] || cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Java: link openjdk into the system JVM path
if [ ! -e /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk ]; then
  sudo ln -sfn "$(brew --prefix)/opt/openjdk/libexec/openjdk.jdk" \
    /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk
fi

# Claude Code
if ! command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
fi
claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman || true
claude plugin install caveman@caveman || true

# then install toolchains as needed, e.g.: mise use -g node@lts ruby@3.3

# Fisher + fish plugins
if ! fish -c 'functions -q fisher' >/dev/null 2>&1; then

# tmux + oh-my-tmux

content.text:
Done. Three files changed:

- **`config.fish`** (repo) — added mise activation + upgraded `FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND` to include hidden/follow/exclude-.git; dropped legacy Python 3.9 PATH entry.
- **`.zshrc`** (new, in repo) — pipx PATH, fzf sourcing, `FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND`, mise activation, entire completions. Matches your live config plus the fzf addition.
- **`install-core.sh`** — now copies `config.fish` → `~/.config/fish/config.fish` and `.zshrc` → `~/.zshrc`; exports `$HOME/.local/bin` up top so `mise settings add` works right after the curl install; removed the two "NOTE: add to your shell config" placeholders since the configs are now deployed.

Note: `cp .zshrc ~/.zshrc` overwrites your current `~/.zshrc` on next install run. The repo version is a superset (same pipx/fzf-source/mise/entire lines, plus `FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND`), so nothing is lost — but worth eyeballing before running.

message.content: what do i need to fix on my machine now post these changes

content.text:
Four things, in order:

**1. Deploy the new shell configs** (the repo versions aren't live yet):
```
cd ~/src/dotfiles
cp config.fish ~/.config/fish/config.fish
cp .zshrc ~/.zshrc
```
Then `exec fish` or `exec zsh` to pick up `FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND` and the other env vars. Your current `~/.config/fish/config.fish` is missing the `EDITOR`/`TERM`/`LANG`/`LC_*`/fzf/alias lines — this sync fixes that.

**2. Sanity-check the Brewfile reflects reality**:
```
brew bundle check --file=Brewfile
```
Lists anything declared but not installed. Then:
```
brew bundle cleanup --file=Brewfile
```
This shows packages you have that aren't in the Brewfile — expect `node` and `nvm` to show up (now managed by mise). Add `--force` to actually uninstall them once you're happy with the list.

**3. Install node via mise** (since we dropped brew node/nvm):
```
mise use -g node@lts
```
Also ruby if you want it: `mise use -g ruby@3.3`.

**4. Commit the repo changes**: new `Brewfile` + new `.zshrc` + modified `config.fish` / `install-core.sh`. `git status` in `~/src/dotfiles` will show them.

One thing to eyeball before step 1: `diff ~/.zshrc ~/src/dotfiles/.zshrc` — confirm nothing personal is getting overwritten.

message.content: what about the oh-my-fish changes you suggested

content.text:
Right — I punted on that one because replacing a prompt is an aesthetic call and you didn't confirm. Two reasonable swaps:

- **starship** — works in both fish and zsh, so one prompt config across both shells. Given you use both, this is the stronger pick. `brew install starship`, then add `starship init fish | source` to `config.fish` and `eval "$(starship init zsh)"` to `.zshrc`.
- **tide** — fish-only, faster, installed via fisher. Nicer out of the box but doesn't help your zsh side.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")"

# pipx and mise install into ~/.local/bin; make sure it's on PATH for this script
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

# Homebrew packages (see Brewfile)
brew update
brew bundle --file=Brewfile

# Python tooling
pipx ensurepath
pipx install poetry
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish/completions
poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish

# Shell configs
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish
cp config.fish ~/.config/fish/config.fish
cp .zshrc ~/.zshrc

# Neovim config (lazy.nvim auto-installs on first launch)
mkdir -p ~/.config/nvim
cp init.lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -rf ~/.config/nvim/lua
cp -R lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -f ~/.config/nvim/init.vim

# mise: Ruby/Node/Python runtime manager
if ! command -v mise >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl https://mise.run | sh
fi
mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby || true
# mise activation is configured in config.fish / .zshrc — open a new shell,
# then install toolchains as needed, e.g.: mise use -g node@lts ruby@3.3

# Fisher + fish plugins
if ! fish -c 'functions -q fisher' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  fish -c 'curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source; and fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher'
fi
fish -c 'fisher install jethrokuan/z'

# Oh-My-Fish (for the `clearance` theme)
if [ ! -d ~/.local/share/omf ]; then
  curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oh-my-fish/oh-my-fish/master/bin/install | fish
fi
fish -c 'omf install clearance; and omf theme clearance'

# fzf keybindings & shell integration (FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND is set in config.fish / .zshrc)
"$(brew --prefix)"/opt/fzf/install --all

# tmux + oh-my-tmux
[ -d ~/oh-my-tmux ] || git clone https://github.com/gpakosz/.tmux.git ~/oh-my-tmux
ln -sf ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
[ -f ~/.tmux.conf.local ] || cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Java: link openjdk into the system JVM path
if [ ! -e /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk ]; then
  sudo ln -sfn "$(brew --prefix)/opt/openjdk/libexec/openjdk.jdk" \
    /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk
fi

# Claude Code
if ! command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
fi
claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman || true
claude plugin install caveman@caveman || true

content.text:
Done. Changes:

- **`Brewfile`**: added `brew "starship"`.
- **`config.fish`**: `starship init fish | source` after mise.
- **`.zshrc`**: `eval "$(starship init zsh)"` after mise.
- **`install-core.sh`**: removed the oh-my-fish install + clearance theme block entirely.

To clean up your machine:
```
brew install starship
# redeploy configs:
cp config.fish ~/.config/fish/config.fish
cp .zshrc ~/.zshrc
# uninstall omf (optional, once you've confirmed starship works):
rm -rf ~/.local/share/omf ~/.config/omf
# drop the clearance fisher plugin if it's there:
fish -c 'fisher remove oh-my-fish/theme-clearance' 2>/dev/null || true
exec fish
```

Starship works with sensible defaults out of the box. If you want to tune it, config lives at `~/.config/starship.toml` — worth adding that to the dotfiles repo once you've settled on preferences.

content.text:
Two leftover fish-autoload files are causing this. Run:

```
rm ~/.config/fish/conf.d/omf.fish
fish -c 'fisher remove jorgebucaran/nvm.fish'
exec fish
```

- `conf.d/omf.fish` is the OMF bootstrap that tried to `source` the now-deleted `~/.local/share/omf/init.fish` — delete it.
- `conf.d/nvm.fish` belongs to the jorgebucaran/nvm.fish fisher plugin (also redundant now that mise handles node). `fisher remove` cleans both the plugin file and the `fish_plugins` entry.

Also worth noting for your dotfiles: the install-core.sh still does `fisher install jethrokuan/z` but nothing removes old plugins — if you add/drop plugins later, the install script won't converge. A cleaner pattern is to commit `fish_plugins` to the repo and have install-core.sh do `fish -c 'fisher update'`, which installs/removes to match that file exactly. Want me to switch to that?

# Python tooling
pipx ensurepath
pipx install poetry
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish/completions
poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish

# Shell configs
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish
cp config.fish ~/.config/fish/config.fish
cp .zshrc ~/.zshrc

# Neovim config (lazy.nvim auto-installs on first launch)
mkdir -p ~/.config/nvim
cp init.lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -rf ~/.config/nvim/lua
cp -R lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -f ~/.config/nvim/init.vim

# mise settings (mise itself is installed via Brewfile)
mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby || true
# mise activation is configured in config.fish / .zshrc — open a new shell,
# then install toolchains as needed, e.g.: mise use -g node@lts ruby@3.3

# Fisher + fish plugins
if ! fish -c 'functions -q fisher' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  fish -c 'curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source; and fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher'
fi
fish -c 'fisher install jethrokuan/z'

# fzf keybindings & shell integration (FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND is set in config.fish / .zshrc)
"$(brew --prefix)"/opt/fzf/install --all

# tmux + oh-my-tmux
[ -d ~/oh-my-tmux ] || git clone https://github.com/gpakosz/.tmux.git ~/oh-my-tmux
ln -sf ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
[ -f ~/.tmux.conf.local ] || cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Java: link openjdk into the system JVM path
if [ ! -e /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk ]; then
  sudo ln -sfn "$(brew --prefix)/opt/openjdk/libexec/openjdk.jdk" \
    /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk
fi

# Claude Code
if ! command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
fi
claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman || true
claude plugin install caveman@caveman || true

lines: # mise activation is configured in config.fish / .zshrc — open a new shell,
# then install toolchains as needed, e.g.: mise use -g node@lts ruby@3.3
+# Fisher + fish plugins (plugin set is managed via fish_plugins in this repo)
fish -c 'curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source; and fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher'
# fzf keybindings & shell integration (FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND is set in config.fish / .zshrc)

content.text:
Done.

- **`fish_plugins`** (new, in repo) — just `jorgebucaran/fisher` and `jethrokuan/z`.
- **`install-core.sh`** — after bootstrapping fisher, copies `fish_plugins` into `~/.config/fish/` and runs `fisher update`. Now the repo file is the source of truth: add a line → next install pulls it in; remove a line → next install uninstalls it.

To reconcile your live machine right now:
```
cd ~/src/dotfiles
cp fish_plugins ~/.config/fish/fish_plugins
fish -c 'fisher update'
```
That'll remove `jorgebucaran/nvm.fish` (not in the new file) and its `conf.d/nvm.fish` in one shot — so you can skip the separate `fisher remove` command I suggested earlier.

content.text: Hold on — the diff exposes a regression I introduced. My rewrite of `install-core.sh` silently dropped the Claude Code tmux notification install block (`cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh …`, `chmod +x`, and the Python that patches `~/.claude/settings.json` with the Stop hook). Those are real features from recent commits. Let me restore them before we commit.

input.old_string:
claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman || true
claude plugin install caveman@caveman || true
input.new_string:
claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman || true
claude plugin install caveman@caveman || true

# Claude Code tmux notification scripts
mkdir -p ~/.claude/scripts
cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh ~/.claude/scripts/
cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh ~/.claude/scripts/
chmod +x ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh

# Register the Stop hook in ~/.claude/settings.json (idempotent)
python3 - <<'PYEOF'
import json, os

path = os.path.expanduser("~/.claude/settings.json")
settings = {}
if os.path.exists(path):
    with open(path) as f:
        settings = json.load(f)

hook = {"type": "command", "command": "bash ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh"}
stop_hooks = settings.setdefault("hooks", {}).setdefault("Stop", [])

if not any(h.get("hooks", []) == [hook] for h in stop_hooks):
    stop_hooks.append({"hooks": [hook]})

with open(path, "w") as f:
    json.dump(settings, f, indent=2)
    f.write("\n")

print("Claude settings.json updated with tmux Stop hook")
PYEOF

import json, os

if os.path.exists(path):
    with open(path) as f:

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")"

# pipx and mise install into ~/.local/bin; make sure it's on PATH for this script
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

# Homebrew packages (see Brewfile)
brew update
brew bundle --file=Brewfile

# Python tooling
pipx ensurepath
pipx install poetry
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish/completions
poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish

# Shell configs
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish
cp config.fish ~/.config/fish/config.fish
cp .zshrc ~/.zshrc

# Neovim config (lazy.nvim auto-installs on first launch)
mkdir -p ~/.config/nvim
cp init.lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -rf ~/.config/nvim/lua
cp -R lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -f ~/.config/nvim/init.vim

# mise settings (mise itself is installed via Brewfile)
mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby || true
# mise activation is configured in config.fish / .zshrc — open a new shell,
# then install toolchains as needed, e.g.: mise use -g node@lts ruby@3.3

# Fisher + fish plugins (plugin set is managed via fish_plugins in this repo)
if ! fish -c 'functions -q fisher' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  fish -c 'curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source; and fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher'
fi
cp fish_plugins ~/.config/fish/fish_plugins
fish -c 'fisher update'

# fzf keybindings & shell integration (FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND is set in config.fish / .zshrc)
"$(brew --prefix)"/opt/fzf/install --all

# tmux + oh-my-tmux
[ -d ~/oh-my-tmux ] || git clone https://github.com/gpakosz/.tmux.git ~/oh-my-tmux
ln -sf ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
[ -f ~/.tmux.conf.local ] || cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Java: link openjdk into the system JVM path
if [ ! -e /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk ]; then
  sudo ln -sfn "$(brew --prefix)/opt/openjdk/libexec/openjdk.jdk" \
    /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk
fi

# Claude Code
if ! command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
fi
claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman || true
claude plugin install caveman@caveman || true

input.prompt:
Fetch the README of https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk and report in under 150 words:

1. What is rtk — is it a CLI, a Claude Code plugin, or a Claude Code plugin marketplace / meta-tool?
2. How is it installed (confirm `brew install rtk` is the official path)?
3. After install, what command(s) does a user run — does it register itself with Claude Code automatically, or do they need to run `claude plugin ...` commands or equivalent?
4. What relationship does rtk have to Claude Code settings.json / hooks / agents — does it write to `~/.claude/` files?

Context: user wants to add rtk to a dotfiles install script alongside other Claude Code setup steps (`claude plugin marketplace add`, `claude plugin install`, hook registration). I need to know what install-core.sh needs to actually *do* for rtk beyond the already-present brew install — nothing more, or additional commands.

Please use WebFetch on https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk to read the README.

content.text:
I have enough for a useful answer.

---

**rtk summary**

1. **What it is:** A standalone **CLI proxy tool** — not a Claude Code plugin, not a marketplace. It filters/compresses command output (e.g. rewrites `git status` to `rtk git status`) before it reaches the LLM context.

2. **Install:** Yes, `brew install rtk` is the official recommended method (Homebrew listed first; cargo, install scripts, and binaries are alternatives).

3. **Post-install, the user must run:**
   ```
   rtk init -g
   ```
   It does **not** auto-register. The `-g` is the global flag. After init, **restart Claude Code**. No `claude plugin ...` commands are involved — rtk integrates via Claude Code's hook system, not the plugin system.

4. **Relationship to `~/.claude/`:** `rtk init -g` writes a **PreToolUse hook entry into Claude Code's settings.json** (the uninstall step explicitly says "Remove hook, RTK.md, settings.json entry"), plus drops an `RTK.md` file and its own config under `~/.config/rtk/`.

**What install-core.sh needs beyond `brew install rtk`:** one line — `rtk init -g` (idempotent init). That's it; no plugin marketplace/install commands needed.

content.text:
Here's what I found, and the proposed structure.

**Current state:**
- `install-core.sh` handles three Claude Code concerns: CLI install, caveman plugin (via marketplace), tmux Stop hook (via a Python patch of `~/.claude/settings.json`).
- `~/.claude/settings.json` currently holds `enabledPlugins` + `extraKnownMarketplaces` — these are managed by `claude` commands themselves, not by you.
- `~/.claude/settings.local.json` doesn't exist yet.
- `~/src/nestl/.claude/settings.local.json` has the `env` + permissions allow list you want as the machine default.
- `rtk` is a standalone CLI that filters/compresses command output before it reaches the LLM. Install is `brew install rtk` (already in Brewfile) + `rtk init -g` (writes a PreToolUse hook into `~/.claude/settings.json`, idempotent).

**Proposed structure:**

1. **Commit `claude/settings.local.json`** in the dotfiles repo — that's the nestl permissions + env, plus the tmux Stop hook baked in. One declarative file replaces the Python patch.
2. **install-core.sh Claude Code block becomes:**
   - `claude` CLI install (curl, guarded)
   - `rtk init -g || true` (registers rtk's hook into `settings.json`)
   - `claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman` + `claude plugin install caveman@caveman` (caveman)
   - `cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-*.sh ~/.claude/scripts/` (unchanged)
   - `cp claude/settings.local.json ~/.claude/settings.local.json` (replaces the Python patch)
3. **Delete the Python heredoc** — no longer needed because the Stop hook ships baked into the committed settings.local.json.

One decision: the copy of `settings.local.json` — unconditional overwrite, or "only if missing"? Your shell configs overwrite; `.tmux.conf.local` is "only if missing". I'd go **unconditional** for settings.local.json so the repo stays the source of truth — you add a new allow rule by editing the repo file, commit, re-run install. If you prefer guarded (so local tweaks survive), tell me and I'll flip it.

Want me to apply this?

file.content:
# unbind C-b
# set -g prefix C-a
# bind C-a send-prefix

# if you don't want Oh my tmux! to alter a binding or a setting, use #!important
# bind c new-window -c '#{pane_current_path}' #!important

# display a message after toggling mouse support
bind m run "cut -c3- '#{TMUX_CONF}' | sh -s _toggle_mouse" \; display 'mouse #{?#{mouse},on,off}'

# -- Claude Code session notifications -----------------------------------------

# Clear Claude ready indicator when switching windows/panes (you're now looking at it)
# Use -gu first to avoid duplicates on config reload
set-hook -gu after-select-window
set-hook -gu after-select-pane
set-hook -gu client-session-changed
set-hook -gu session-closed
set-hook -ga after-select-window 'run-shell "bash ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh #{pane_id}"'
set-hook -ga after-select-pane 'run-shell "bash ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh #{pane_id}"'
set-hook -ga client-session-changed 'run-shell "bash ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh #{pane_id}"'

# move status line to top
#set -g status-position top


# -- tpm -----------------------------------------------------------------------

# while I don't use tpm myself, many people requested official support so here
# is a seamless integration that automatically installs plugins in parallel

stop_hooks = settings.setdefault("hooks", {}).setdefault("Stop", [])

    stop_hooks.append({"hooks": [hook]})

    json.dump(settings, f, indent=2)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")"

# pipx and mise install into ~/.local/bin; make sure it's on PATH for this script
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

# Homebrew packages (see Brewfile)
brew update
brew bundle --file=Brewfile

# Python tooling
pipx ensurepath
pipx install poetry
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish/completions
poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish

# Shell configs
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish
cp config.fish ~/.config/fish/config.fish
cp .zshrc ~/.zshrc

# Neovim config (lazy.nvim auto-installs on first launch)
mkdir -p ~/.config/nvim
cp init.lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -rf ~/.config/nvim/lua
cp -R lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -f ~/.config/nvim/init.vim

# mise settings (mise itself is installed via Brewfile)
mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby || true
# mise activation is configured in config.fish / .zshrc — open a new shell,
# then install toolchains as needed, e.g.: mise use -g node@lts ruby@3.3

# Fisher + fish plugins (plugin set is managed via fish_plugins in this repo)
if ! fish -c 'functions -q fisher' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  fish -c 'curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source; and fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher'
fi
cp fish_plugins ~/.config/fish/fish_plugins
fish -c 'fisher update'

# fzf keybindings & shell integration (FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND is set in config.fish / .zshrc)
"$(brew --prefix)"/opt/fzf/install --all

# tmux + oh-my-tmux
[ -d ~/oh-my-tmux ] || git clone https://github.com/gpakosz/.tmux.git ~/oh-my-tmux
ln -sf ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
[ -f ~/.tmux.conf.local ] || cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Java: link openjdk into the system JVM path
if [ ! -e /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk ]; then
  sudo ln -sfn "$(brew --prefix)/opt/openjdk/libexec/openjdk.jdk" \
    /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk
fi

# Claude Code
if ! command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
fi
claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman || true
claude plugin install caveman@caveman || true

# Claude Code tmux notification scripts
mkdir -p ~/.claude/scripts
cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh ~/.claude/scripts/
cp claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh ~/.claude/scripts/
chmod +x ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh

# Register the Stop hook in ~/.claude/settings.json (idempotent)
python3 - <<'PYEOF'
import json, os

path = os.path.expanduser("~/.claude/settings.json")
settings = {}
if os.path.exists(path):
    with open(path) as f:
        settings = json.load(f)

hook = {"type": "command", "command": "bash ~/.claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh"}
stop_hooks = settings.setdefault("hooks", {}).setdefault("Stop", [])

if not any(h.get("hooks", []) == [hook] for h in stop_hooks):
    stop_hooks.append({"hooks": [hook]})

with open(path, "w") as f:
    json.dump(settings, f, indent=2)
    f.write("\n")

print("Claude settings.json updated with tmux Stop hook")
PYEOF

content.text:
All changes in place. Summary:

**Repo changes:**
- New: `claude/settings.local.json` — env + permissions allow list (terminal-notifier entry removed).
- Modified: `install-core.sh` — new Claude Code block: CLI install, `rtk init -g`, caveman plugin, conditional cp of settings.local.json.
- Modified: `.tmux.conf.local` — removed the Claude Code session notifications hook block.
- Deleted: `claude/scripts/tmux-claude-notify.sh`, `claude/scripts/tmux-claude-clear.sh`.

**Live machine cleanup you need to do manually** (install script won't do it):
```
rm -rf ~/.claude/scripts
cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local   # force-redeploy to drop the old hooks
tmux source ~/.tmux.conf.local           # reload in running tmux, or just restart tmux
rtk init -g                              # register rtk's PreToolUse hook now
[ -f ~/.claude/settings.local.json ] || cp claude/settings.local.json ~/.claude/settings.local.json
```

The settings.local.json copy is guarded because you asked for "only if missing" — if you already have one locally, it won't overwrite. Change the repo file and `cp` manually when you want to update.

Want me to commit this?

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")"

# pipx and mise install into ~/.local/bin; make sure it's on PATH for this script
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

# Homebrew packages (see Brewfile)
brew update
brew bundle --file=Brewfile

# Python tooling
pipx ensurepath
pipx install poetry
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish/completions
poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish

# Shell configs
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish
cp config.fish ~/.config/fish/config.fish
cp .zshrc ~/.zshrc

# Neovim config (lazy.nvim auto-installs on first launch)
mkdir -p ~/.config/nvim
cp init.lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -rf ~/.config/nvim/lua
cp -R lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -f ~/.config/nvim/init.vim

# mise settings (mise itself is installed via Brewfile)
mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby || true
# mise activation is configured in config.fish / .zshrc — open a new shell,
# then install toolchains as needed, e.g.: mise use -g node@lts ruby@3.3

# Fisher + fish plugins (plugin set is managed via fish_plugins in this repo)
if ! fish -c 'functions -q fisher' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  fish -c 'curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source; and fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher'
fi
cp fish_plugins ~/.config/fish/fish_plugins
fish -c 'fisher update'

# fzf keybindings & shell integration (FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND is set in config.fish / .zshrc)
"$(brew --prefix)"/opt/fzf/install --all

# tmux + oh-my-tmux
[ -d ~/oh-my-tmux ] || git clone https://github.com/gpakosz/.tmux.git ~/oh-my-tmux
ln -sf ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
[ -f ~/.tmux.conf.local ] || cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Java: link openjdk into the system JVM path
if [ ! -e /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk ]; then
  sudo ln -sfn "$(brew --prefix)/opt/openjdk/libexec/openjdk.jdk" \
    /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk
fi

# Claude Code CLI
if ! command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
fi

# rtk (LLM output filter) — registers its own PreToolUse hook in ~/.claude/settings.json
rtk init -g || true

# Caveman plugin
claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman || true
claude plugin install caveman@caveman || true

# Global Claude Code user settings (permissions + env) — only install if missing
mkdir -p ~/.claude
[ -f ~/.claude/settings.local.json ] || cp claude/settings.local.json ~/.claude/settings.local.json

claude plugin marketplace add JuliusBrussee/caveman || true

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")"

# pipx and mise install into ~/.local/bin; make sure it's on PATH for this script
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

# Homebrew packages (see Brewfile)
brew update
brew bundle --file=Brewfile

# Python tooling
pipx ensurepath
pipx install poetry
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish/completions
poetry completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/poetry.fish

# Shell configs
mkdir -p ~/.config/fish
cp config.fish ~/.config/fish/config.fish
cp .zshrc ~/.zshrc

# Neovim config (lazy.nvim auto-installs on first launch)
mkdir -p ~/.config/nvim
cp init.lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -rf ~/.config/nvim/lua
cp -R lua ~/.config/nvim/
rm -f ~/.config/nvim/init.vim

# mise settings (mise itself is installed via Brewfile)
mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby || true
# mise activation is configured in config.fish / .zshrc — open a new shell,
# then install toolchains as needed, e.g.: mise use -g node@lts ruby@3.3

# Fisher + fish plugins (plugin set is managed via fish_plugins in this repo)
if ! fish -c 'functions -q fisher' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  fish -c 'curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source; and fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher'
fi
cp fish_plugins ~/.config/fish/fish_plugins
fish -c 'fisher update'

# fzf keybindings & shell integration (FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND is set in config.fish / .zshrc)
"$(brew --prefix)"/opt/fzf/install --all

# tmux + oh-my-tmux

ln -sf ~/oh-my-tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
[ -f ~/.tmux.conf.local ] || cp .tmux.conf.local ~/.tmux.conf.local

# Java: link openjdk into the system JVM path
if [ ! -e /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk ]; then

    /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk.jdk

claude plugin install agent-skills@addy-agent-skills || true

content.text:
Full idempotency audit:

| Block | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `brew update` + `brew bundle` | ✅ | bundle skips already-installed |
| `pipx ensurepath` | ✅ | only edits config if missing |
| `pipx install poetry` | ✅ (just fixed) | now guarded by `pipx list` grep |
| poetry fish completions | ✅ | overwrites silently |
| `cp config.fish` / `cp .zshrc` | ⚠️ | unconditional overwrite — if you hand-edit these on a machine, re-running clobbers your edits. Intentional: repo is source of truth |
| nvim config (`rm -rf lua` + `cp -R`) | ✅ | deliberate overwrite |
| `mise settings add … \|\| true` | ✅ | `\|\| true` guard |
| fisher install + fish_plugins + `fisher update` | ✅ | conditional bootstrap, `fisher update` reconciles |
| `fzf/install --all` | ✅ | runs each time but only rewrites its own files |
| oh-my-tmux clone | ✅ | `[ -d ] \|\|` guard |
| tmux symlink (`ln -sf`) | ✅ | `-f` overwrites harmlessly |
| `.tmux.conf.local` | ⚠️ | guarded with `[ -f ]` — if you update the repo version, it **won't** redeploy. Either delete the live file before re-running, or change the guard |
| openjdk symlink | ✅ | `[ ! -e ]` guard; uses sudo so may reprompt |
| claude CLI curl install | ✅ | `command -v` guard |
| `rtk init -g \|\| true` | ✅ | idempotent per rtk docs, guarded anyway |
| claude plugin marketplace/install (4 lines) | ✅ | `\|\| true` guards |
| `~/.claude/settings.local.json` | ⚠️ | `[ -f ] \|\|` guard — same caveat as `.tmux.conf.local`: repo updates won't propagate |

**Summary:** safe to re-run. The three `⚠️` rows aren't bugs, they're tradeoffs:
- **Shell configs overwrite**: aligned with "dotfiles is canonical".
- **`.tmux.conf.local` and `settings.local.json` are guarded**: you asked for only-if-missing so local tweaks survive. Downside: if you bump the repo version, you need to manually redeploy.

Commit the poetry fix?
